scenario: Wren Telco evaluates outage signals on Camunda 8.8 across 4000 incidents per day and stores per-signal booleans in a process variable list such as [false, true]. A modeller writes an exclusive gateway condition that returns true when at least one element of the list is true and returns false on an empty list, using the documented FEEL function and replacement note on the list built-in functions page.
question: Which FEEL list function returns true when any list element is true and which legacy function did it replace per the docs?

options.text: or(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns true if any element of the list is true, returns false on an empty list, accepts the list as a sequence of elements, and is the current FEEL disjunction function on the list built-in functions page on Camunda 8.8

any(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns true if any element of the list is true, returns false on an empty list, accepts the list as a sequence of elements, and replaced the previous function or() per the docs note on the list built-in functions page

some(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns true if any element of the list is true, returns false on an empty list, accepts the list as a sequence of elements, and replaced the previous function or() per the docs note on the list built-in functions page

fold or(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns true if any element of the list is true, returns false on an empty list, accepts the list as a sequence of elements, and replaced the previous function or() per the docs note on the list built-in functions page

a.text: Incorrect. The docs state that or() was replaced by any() and that the previous function should not be used anymore; or() is not the current FEEL disjunction function on the list built-in functions page.

b.text: Correct. The list built-in functions page documents any(list: list<boolean>): boolean. The function returns true if any element of the list is true, returns false on an empty list, and the list parameter can be passed as a list or as a sequence of elements. The page notes that any() replaced the previous or() function, which is now the previous Camunda Extension function.

c.text: Incorrect. The list built-in functions page does not document a some() function; the documented any-true-or-empty function is any(list).

d.text: Incorrect. The list built-in functions page does not document a fold or() function; the documented any-true-or-empty function is any(list).

explanation: any(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns true if any element of the list is true and returns false otherwise, including on an empty list. The list parameter can be passed as a list or as a sequence of elements. The page notes that any() replaced the previous or() function, which should not be used anymore.
